title: metastelma schlechteri
intro: Metastelma schlechteri, also known as the 'Coral Milk Vine', is a flowering plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Metastelma schlechteri include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin infections. The herbal preparation of the plant involves drying and infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
